top_signals.title: Bank of England launches policy statement and draft rules on regulating systemic stablecoins
top_signals.summary: The Bank of England has today published its policy statement and draft Code of Practice (rules) for systemic stablecoin issuers.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Accredited official statistics: GDP deflators at market prices, and money GDP June 2026 (Quarterly National Accounts)
top_signals.summary: This document contains the latest gross domestic product (GDP) deflators. The GDP deflator can be viewed as a measure of general inflation in the domestic economy.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Inflation expectations impacting rate path, bonds and global risk assets.

top_signals.title: UK and France agree with Oman to ensure safety of its territorial waters
top_signals.summary: France said it has deployed mine countermeasures to the Middle East, including two mine-hunting ships.

top_signals.title: Labour defends Burnham’s ‘very powerful’ No 10 North plans
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham’s proposed ‘No 10 North’ would lead to a “very powerful reorganisation of government”, deputy Labour leader Lucy Powell has said. Her words come as ministers push ahead with one of the biggest overhauls of English local government in decades before Burnham is expected to enter Downing Street later this month, with Labour arguing [...]

alerts.title: The 250-year history of U.S. energy consumption
alerts.summary: Over the 250-year history of our nation, energy consumption has evolved from wood use in the 18th and 19th centuries to today's use of modern renewable, hydrocarbon, and nuclear technology. In 2025, total energy used in the United States was 96 quadrillion British thermal units (quads), up 2% from 2024, but below 2007's record 99 quads. Petroleum was the most-used energy source last year, followed closely by natural gas. Use of renewable, coal, and nuclear energy each made up about 9% of total energy use.

alerts.title: Almonty kicks off processing at Sangdong tungsten mine
alerts.summary: The Sangdong mine was historically one of the world’s largest tungsten producers before operations were suspended in the early 1990s.
